You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 1bf9c461).

## Issue to Resolve

0.48.0 does not allow nordic chars √•√§√∂√•√¶√∏ in package-names
When upgrading from 0.47.1 to 0.48.0, ktlint complains on package names containing √•√¶√∏.

## Expected Behavior
Expecting √¶√∏√• to be accepted

## Observed Behavior
` Package name contains a disallowed character (cannot be auto-corrected) (package-name)`

## Steps to Reproduce
Create class `Bar` in package with name `f√∏√∏` and run `mvn antrun:run@ktlint`

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used: 0.48.0
* Relevant parts of the `.editorconfig` settings
* Name and version (or code for custom task) of integration used (Gradle plugin, Maven plugin, command line, custom Gradle task): Apache Maven 3.8.1
* Operating System and version: macOS Ventura 13.1

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

Similar to https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1530
More problems like this are likely to occur in this release. `0.48.0` contains some rules which formerly where experimental but now have been added to the `standard` rules. Also new `experimental` naming rules have been added in `0.48.x`. Can you please run ktlint with `--experimental` once to identify which of the newly added experimental rules also break on nordic characters?
It seems like it also fails on property-, class- and function-names containing nordic characters when running with experimental rules:

`Property name should start with a lowercase letter and use camel case (cannot be auto-corrected) (experimental:property-naming)" on properties containing nordic characters.`
`Class or object name should start with an uppercase letter and use camel case (cannot be auto-corrected) (experimental:class-naming)`
`Function name should start with a lowercase letter (except factory methods) and use camel case (cannot be auto-corrected) (experimental:function-naming)`
Indeed, that is not entirely unexpected. Thnx for confirming though. Sorry for the inconvenience.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-experimental/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/experimental/ClassNamingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-experimental/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/experimental/FunctionNamingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-experimental/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/experimental/PropertyNamingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-experimental/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/experimental/internal/RegExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/PackageNameRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1‚Üípackage com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.experimental
 2‚Üí
 3‚Üíimport com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule
 4‚Üíimport com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType
 5‚Üíimport com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.CLASS
 6‚Üíimport com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.IDENTIFIER
 7‚Üíimport com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.OBJECT_DECLARATION
 8‚Üíimport org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 9‚Üí
10‚Üí/**
11‚Üí * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#naming-rules
12‚Üí *
13‚Üí * The Kotlin convention does not allow explicitly to use backticked class name but it makes sense to allow this as
14‚Üí * well as it is more consistent with name of test functions.
15‚Üí */
16‚Üípublic class ClassNamingRule : Rule("$EXPERIMENTAL_RULE_SET_ID:class-naming") {
17‚Üí    private var allowBacktickedClassName = false
18‚Üí
19‚Üí    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
20‚Üí        node: ASTNode,
21‚Üí        autoCorrect: Boolean,
22‚Üí        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
23‚Üí    ) {
24‚Üí        if (!allowBacktickedClassName && node.elementType == ElementType.IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
25‚Üí            node
26‚Üí                .findChildByType(ElementType.DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION)
27‚Üí                ?.text
28‚Üí                ?.takeIf { it.startsWith("org.junit.jupiter.api") }
29‚Üí                ?.let {
30‚Üí                    // Assume that each file that imports a Junit Jupiter Api class is a test class
31‚Üí                    allowBacktickedClassName = true
32‚Üí                }
33‚Üí        }
34‚Üí
35‚Üí        node
36‚Üí            .takeIf { node.elementType == CLASS || node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION }
37‚Üí            ?.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
38‚Üí            ?.takeUnless { it.isValidFunctionName() || it.isTestClass() }
39‚Üí            ?.let {
40‚Üí                emit(it.startOffset, "Class or object name should start with an uppercase letter and use camel case", false)
41‚Üí            }
42‚Üí    }
43‚Üí
44‚Üí    private fun ASTNode.isValidFunctionName() =
45‚Üí        text.matches(VALID_CLASS_NAME_REGEXP)
46‚Üí
47‚Üí    private fun ASTNode.isTestClass() =
48‚Üí        allowBacktickedClassName && hasBackTickedIdentifier()
49‚Üí
50‚Üí    private fun ASTNode.hasBackTickedIdentifier() =
51‚Üí        text.matches(BACK_TICKED_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP)
52‚Üí
53‚Üí    private companion object {
54‚Üí        val VALID_CLASS_NAME_REGEXP = Regex("[A-Z][A-Za-z\\d]*")
55‚Üí        val BACK_TICKED_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP = Regex("`.*`")
56‚Üí    }
57‚Üí}

1‚Üípackage com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.experimental
 2‚Üí
 3‚Üíimport com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule
 4‚Üíimport com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.FUN
 5‚Üíimport com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.IDENTIFIER
 6‚Üíimport com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType.IMPORT_DIRECTIVE
 7‚Üíimport org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 8‚Üíimport org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtFunction
 9‚Üíimport org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtImportDirective
10‚Üí
11‚Üí/**
12‚Üí * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#function-names
13‚Üí */
14‚Üípublic class FunctionNamingRule : Rule("$EXPERIMENTAL_RULE_SET_ID:function-naming") {
15‚Üí    private var isTestClass = false
16‚Üí
17‚Üí    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
18‚Üí        node: ASTNode,
19‚Üí        autoCorrect: Boolean,
20‚Üí        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
21‚Üí    ) {
22‚Üí        if (!isTestClass && node.elementType == IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
23‚Üí            (node.psi as KtImportDirective)
24‚Üí                .importPath
25‚Üí                ?.pathStr
26‚Üí                ?.takeIf {
27‚Üí                    it.startsWith(ORG_JUNIT) || it.startsWith(ORG_TESTNG) || it.startsWith(KOTLIN_TEST)
28‚Üí                }
29‚Üí                ?.let {
30‚Üí                    // Assume that each file that imports a Junit Jupiter Api class is a test class
31‚Üí                    isTestClass = true
32‚Üí                }
33‚Üí        }
34‚Üí
35‚Üí        node
36‚Üí            .takeIf { node.elementType == FUN }
37‚Üí            ?.takeUnless {
38‚Üí                node.isFactoryMethod() ||
39‚Üí                    node.isTestMethod() ||
40‚Üí                    node.hasValidFunctionName()
41‚Üí            }?.let {
42‚Üí                val identifierOffset =
43‚Üí                    node
44‚Üí                        .findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
45‚Üí                        ?.startOffset
46‚Üí                        ?: 1
47‚Üí                emit(identifierOffset, "Function name should start with a lowercase letter (except factory methods) and use camel case", false)
48‚Üí            }
49‚Üí    }
50‚Üí
51‚Üí    private fun ASTNode.isFactoryMethod() =
52‚Üí        (this.psi as KtFunction)
53‚Üí            .let { it.hasDeclaredReturnType() && it.name == it.typeReference?.text }
54‚Üí
55‚Üí    private fun ASTNode.isTestMethod() =
56‚Üí        isTestClass && hasValidTestFunctionName()
57‚Üí
58‚Üí    private fun ASTNode.hasValidTestFunctionName() =
59‚Üí        findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
60‚Üí            ?.text
61‚Üí            .orEmpty()
62‚Üí            .matches(VALID_TEST_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP)
63‚Üí
64‚Üí    private fun ASTNode.hasValidFunctionName() =
65‚Üí        findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
66‚Üí            ?.text
67‚Üí            .orEmpty()
68‚Üí            .matches(VALID_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP)
69‚Üí
70‚Üí    private companion object {
71‚Üí        val VALID_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP = Regex("[a-z][A-Za-z\\d]*")
72‚Üí        val VALID_TEST_FUNCTION_NAME_REGEXP = Regex("(`.*`)|([a-z][A-Za-z\\d_]*)")
73‚Üí        private const val KOTLIN_TEST = "kotlin.test"
74‚Üí        private const val ORG_JUNIT = "org.junit"
75‚Üí        private const val ORG_TESTNG = "org.testng"
76‚Üí    }
77‚Üí}

1‚Üí# Changelog
  2‚ÜíAll notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3‚ÜíThis project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4‚Üí
  5‚Üí## Unreleased
  6‚Üí
  7‚Üí### Additional clarification on API Changes in `0.48.0` and `0.48.1`
  8‚Üí
  9‚ÜíStarting with Ktlint `0.48.x`, rule and rule sets can be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
 10‚Üí
 11‚ÜíAPI Consumers that provide experimental rules to the KtLintRuleEngine, must also enable the experimental rules or instruct their users to do so in the `.editorconfig` file. From the perspective of the API Consumer it might be confusing or unnecessary to do so as the experimental rule was already provided explicitly.
 12‚Üí
 13‚ÜíKtlint wants to provide the user (e.g. a developer) a uniform and consistent user experience. The `.editorconfig` becomes more and more central to store configuration for Ktlint. This to ensure that all team members use the exact same configuration when running ktlint regardless whether the Ktlint CLI or an API Consumer is being used.
 14‚Üí
 15‚ÜíThe `.editorconfig` is a powerful configuration tool which can be used in very different ways. Most projects use a single `.editorconfig` file containing one common section for kotlin and kotlin scripts files. For example, the `.editorconfig` file of the Ktlint project contains following section:
 16‚Üí```editorconfig
 17‚Üí[*.{kt,kts}]
 18‚Üíij_kotlin_imports_layout = *
 19‚Üíij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma = true
 20‚Üíij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = true
 21‚Üí```
 22‚ÜíOther projects might contain multiple `.editorconfig` files for different parts of the project directory hierarchy. Or, use a single `.editorconfig` file containing multiple sections with different globs. Like all other configuration settings in Ktlint, the user should be able to enable and disable the experimental rules. Both for the entire set of experimental rules and for individual experimental rules.
 23‚Üí
 24‚ÜíKtlint allows API Consumers to set default values and override values for the `.editorconfig`. Specifying a default value means that the user does not need to define the property in the `.editorconfig` file but if the user specifies the value, it will take precedence. Specifying the override value ensures that this takes precedence on a value specified by the user in the `.editorconfig`.
 25‚Üí
 26‚ÜíFrom the Ktlint perspective, it is advised that API Consumers provide the default value. See example below, for how to specify the `editorConfigDefault` property:
 27‚Üí```
 28‚ÜíKtLintRuleEngine(
 29‚Üí    ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
 30‚Üí    editorConfigDefaults = EditorConfigDefaults(
 31‚Üí        EditorConfig
 32‚Üí            .builder()
 33‚Üí            .section(
 34‚Üí                Section
 35‚Üí                    .builder()
 36‚Üí                    .glob(Glob("*.{kt,kts}"))
 37‚Üí                    .properties(
 38‚Üí                        Property
 39‚Üí                            .builder()
 40‚Üí                            .name("ktlint_experimental")
 41‚Üí                            .value("enabled"),
 42‚Üí                    ),
 43‚Üí            )
 44‚Üí            .build()
 45‚Üí    )
 46‚Üí)
 47‚Üí```
 48‚ÜíIf the user has set property `ktlint_experimental` explicitly than that value will be used. If the value is not defined, the value provided via `editorConfigDefaults` will be used.
 49‚Üí
 50‚ÜíIf you do want to ignore the value of `ktlint_experimental` as set by the user, than you can set the EditorConfigOverride property. But as said before that is discouraged as the user might not understand why the `.editorconfig` property is being ignored (provided that the value set is not equal to the value provided by the API Consumer).
 51‚Üí
 52‚Üí### Added
 53‚Üí
 54‚Üí### Removed
 55‚Üí
 56‚Üí### Fixed
 57‚Üí* Fix with array-syntax annotations on the same line as other annotations `annotation` ([#1765](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1765))
 58‚Üí* Do not enable the experimental rules by default when `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` or `ktlint_disabled_rules` are set. ([#1771](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1771))
 59‚Üí* A function signature not having any parameters which exceeds the `max-line-length` should be ignored by rule `function-signature` ([#1773](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1773))
 60‚Üí* Prevent violation of `file-name` rule on code snippets ([#1768](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1768))
 61‚Üí* Clarify that API Consumers have to enable experimental rules ([#1768](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1768))
 62‚Üí* Trim spaces in the `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_imports_layout`'s entries ([#1770](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1770))
 63‚Üí
 64‚Üí### Changed
 65‚Üí
 66‚Üí## [0.48.1] - 2023-01-03
 67‚Üí
 68‚Üí### Added
 69‚Üí
 70‚Üí### Removed
 71‚Üí
 72‚Üí### Fixed
 73‚Üí
 74‚Üí* An enumeration class having a primary constructor and in which the list of enum entries is followed by a semicolon then do not remove the semicolon in case it is followed by code element `no-semi` ([#1733](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1733))

 85‚Üí## [0.48.0] - 2022-12-15
 86‚Üí
 87‚Üí### Indent rule
 88‚Üí
 89‚ÜíThe `indent` rule has been rewritten from scratch. Solving problems in the old algorithm was very difficult. With the new algorithm this becomes a lot easier. Although the new implementation of the rule has been compared against several open source projects containing over 400,000 lines of code, it is still likely that new issues will be discovered. Please report your indentation issues so that these can be fixed as well.
 90‚Üí
 91‚Üí### `.editorconfig` property to disable rules
 92‚Üí
 93‚ÜíIn the previous release (0.47.x), the `.editorconfig`  property `disabled_rules` was deprecated and replaced with `ktlint_disabled_rules`. This latter property has now been deprecated as well in favour of a more flexible and better maintainable solution. Rule and rule sets can now be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
 94‚Üí
 95‚ÜíThe KtLint CLI has not been changed. Although you can still use parameter `--experimental` to enable KtLint's Experimental rule set, you might want to set `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental = enabled` instead.
 96‚Üí
 97‚Üí### API Changes & RuleSet providers
 98‚Üí
 99‚ÜíIf you are not an API consumer or Rule Set provider then you can skip this section.
100‚Üí
101‚Üí#### Class relocations
102‚Üí
103‚ÜíClasses below have been relocated:
104‚Üí
105‚Üí* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties.EditorConfigProperty` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty`. 
106‚Üí* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLintParseException` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintParseException`.
107‚Üí* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleExecutionException` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintRuleException`.
108‚Üí* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.format.internal.Color` has been moved to `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.format.Color`.
109‚Üí* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain.internal.Color` has been moved to `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain.Color`.
110‚Üí
111‚Üí#### Invoking `lint` and `format`
112‚Üí
113‚ÜíThis is the last release that supports the `ExperimentalParams` to invoke the `lint` and `format` functions of KtLint. The `ExperimentalParams` contains a mix of configuration settings which are not dependent on the file/code which is to be processed. Other parameters in that class describe the code/file to be processed but can be configured inconsistently (for example a file with name "foo.kt" could be marked as a Kotlin Script file).
114‚Üí
115‚ÜíThe static object `KtLint` is deprecated and replaced by class `KtLintRuleEngine` which is configured with `KtLintRuleEngineConfiguration`. The instance of the `KtLintRuleEngine` is intended to be reused for scanning all files in a project and should not be recreated per file.
116‚Üí
117‚ÜíBoth `lint` and `format` are simplified and can now be called for a code block or for an entire file.
118‚Üí
119‚Üí```kotlin
120‚Üíimport java.io.File
121‚Üí
122‚Üí// Define a reusable instance of the KtLint Rule Engine
123‚Üíval ktLintRuleEngine = KtLintRuleEngine(
124‚Üí  // Define configuration
125‚Üí)
126‚Üí
127‚Üí
128‚Üí// Process a collection of files
129‚Üíval files: Set<File> // Collect files in a convenient way
130‚Üífiles.forEach(file in files) {
131‚Üí    ktLintRuleEngine.lint(file) {
132‚Üí        // Handle lint violations
133‚Üí    }
134‚Üí}
135‚Üí
136‚Üí// or process a code sample for a given filepath
137‚ÜíktLintRuleEngine.lint(
138‚Üí  code = "code to be linted",
139‚Üí  filePath = Path("/path/to/source/file")
140‚Üí) {
141‚Üí  // Handle lint violations
142‚Üí}
143‚Üí```
144‚Üí
145‚Üí#### Retrieve `.editorconfig`s
146‚Üí
147‚ÜíThe list of `.editorconfig` files which will be accessed by KtLint when linting or formatting a given path can now be retrieved with the new API `KtLint.editorConfigFilePaths(path: Path): List<Path>`. 
148‚Üí
149‚ÜíThis API can be called with either a file or a directory. It's intended usage is that it is called once with the root directory of a project before actually linting or formatting files of that project. When called with a directory path, all `.editorconfig` files in the directory or any of its subdirectories (except hidden directories) are returned. In case the given directory does not contain an `.editorconfig` file or if it does not contain the `root=true` setting, the parent directories are scanned as well until a root `.editorconfig` file is found.
150‚Üí
151‚ÜíCalling this API with a file path results in the `.editorconfig` files that will be accessed when processing that specific file. In case the directory in which the file resides does not contain an `.editorconfig` file or if it does not contain the `root=true` setting, the parent directories are scanned until a root `.editorconfig` file is found.
152‚Üí
153‚Üí#### Psi filename replaces FILE_PATH_USER_DATA_KEY
154‚Üí
155‚ÜíConstant `KtLint.FILE_PATH_USER_DATA_KEY` is deprecated and will be removed in KtLint version 0.49.0. The file name will be passed correctly to the node with element type FILE and can be retrieved as follows:
156‚Üí```kotlin
157‚Üíif (node.isRoot()) {
158‚Üí    val fileName = (node.psi as? KtFile)?.name
159‚Üí    ...
160‚Üí}
161‚Üí```

 20‚Üí/**
 21‚Üí * [Kotlin lang documentation](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#source-file-names):
 22‚Üí * If a Kotlin file contains a single class (potentially with related top-level declarations), its name should be
 23‚Üí * the same as the name of the class, with the `.kt` extension appended. If a file contains multiple classes,
 24‚Üí * or only top-level declarations, choose a name describing what the file contains, and name the file accordingly.
 25‚Üí * Use upper camel case with an uppercase first letter (also known as Pascal case),
 26‚Üí * for example, `ProcessDeclarations.kt`.
 27‚Üí *
 28‚Üí * According to issue https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KTIJ-21897/Kotlin-coding-convention-file-naming-for-class,
 29‚Üí * "class" above should be read as any type of class (data class, enum class, sealed class) and interfaces.
 30‚Üí *
 31‚Üí * A strict implementation of guideline above had unwanted consequences:
 32‚Üí *   - If the file contains a single top level private class, it does not make sense to force the name of file to be
 33‚Üí *     identical to that class.
 34‚Üí *   - If the file contains a coherent set of functions and one of those function returns an instance of a public class
 35‚Üí *     which happens to be the only top level class in that file, it might not always be best to force the file to be
 36‚Üí *     named after that class.
 37‚Üí *   - Existing functionality regarding files containing a single top level object/typealias was lost.
 38‚Üí *
 39‚Üí * Exceptions to this rule:
 40‚Üí * - file without `.kt` extension
 41‚Üí * - file with name `package.kt`
 42‚Üí */
 43‚Üípublic class FilenameRule : Rule("filename") {
 44‚Üí    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 45‚Üí        node: ASTNode,
 46‚Üí        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 47‚Üí        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 48‚Üí    ) {
 49‚Üí        if (node.isRoot()) {
 50‚Üí            node as FileASTNode? ?: error("node is not ${FileASTNode::class} but ${node::class}")
 51‚Üí
 52‚Üí            val filePath = (node.psi as? KtFile)?.virtualFilePath
 53‚Üí            if (filePath?.endsWith(".kt") != true || filePath.endsWith("package.kt")) {
 54‚Üí                // ignore all non ".kt" files (including ".kts")
 55‚Üí                stopTraversalOfAST()
 56‚Üí                return
 57‚Üí            }
 58‚Üí
 59‚Üí            val fileName =
 60‚Üí                filePath
 61‚Üí                    .replace('\\', '/') // Ensure compatibility with Windows OS
 62‚Üí                    .substringAfterLast("/")
 63‚Üí                    .substringBefore(".")
 64‚Üí
 65‚Üí            val topLevelClassDeclarations = node.topLevelDeclarations(CLASS)
 66‚Üí            if (topLevelClassDeclarations.size == 1) {
 67‚Üí                val topLevelClassDeclaration = topLevelClassDeclarations.first()
 68‚Üí                if (node.hasTopLevelDeclarationNotExtending(topLevelClassDeclaration.identifier)) {
 69‚Üí                    fileName.shouldMatchPascalCase(emit)
 70‚Üí                } else {

 72‚Üí                    // that class, then its filename should be identical to the class name.
 73‚Üí                    fileName.shouldMatchClassName(topLevelClassDeclaration.identifier, emit)
 74‚Üí                }
 75‚Üí            } else {
 76‚Üí                val topLevelDeclarations = node.topLevelDeclarations()
 77‚Üí                if (topLevelDeclarations.size == 1) {
 78‚Üí                    val topLevelDeclaration = topLevelDeclarations.first()
 79‚Üí                    if (topLevelDeclaration.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ||
 80‚Üí                        topLevelDeclaration.elementType == TYPEALIAS
 81‚Üí                    ) {
 82‚Üí                        val pascalCaseIdentifier =
 83‚Üí                            topLevelDeclaration
 84‚Üí                                .identifier
 85‚Üí                                .toPascalCase()

 87‚Üí                    } else {

 89‚Üí                    }
 90‚Üí                } else {
 91‚Üí                    fileName.shouldMatchPascalCase(emit)
 92‚Üí                }
 93‚Üí            }
 94‚Üí            stopTraversalOfAST()
 95‚Üí        }
 96‚Üí    }

 98‚Üí    private fun ASTNode.topLevelDeclarations(elementType: IElementType? = null): List<TopLevelDeclaration> =
 99‚Üí        children()
100‚Üí            .filter { elementType == null || it.elementType == elementType }
101‚Üí            .filter { it.doesNotHavePrivateModifier() }
102‚Üí            .mapNotNull { it.toTopLevelDeclaration() }
103‚Üí            .distinct()
104‚Üí            .toList()

107‚Üí        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
108‚Üí            ?.children()
109‚Üí            ?.none { it.text == "private" }
110‚Üí            ?: true

Now I understand the issue. The regex patterns in the rules don't support Unicode letters with diacritics (like Nordic characters √•, √¶, √∏, etc.). The solution already exists in `EnumEntryNameCaseRule.kt` and `FilenameRule.kt` which use the helper function `regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()` to transform regex patterns to support Unicode letters.

Let me check the existing tests for PackageNameRule:

content.text:
Now I understand the issue clearly. The regex patterns in `PackageNameRule`, `ClassNamingRule`, `FunctionNamingRule`, and `PropertyNamingRule` only match ASCII letters (a-z, A-Z), not Unicode letters with diacritics like Nordic characters (√•, √¶, √∏).

The solution already exists in the codebase - the helper function `regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()` in `RegExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters.kt` which transforms regex patterns to use Unicode character classes (`\p{Lu}` for uppercase letters, `\p{Ll}` for lowercase letters). This is already used in `EnumEntryNameCaseRule` and `FilenameRule`.

1‚Üí# Changelog
  2‚ÜíAll notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3‚ÜíThis project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4‚Üí
  5‚Üí## Unreleased
  6‚Üí
  7‚Üí### Additional clarification on API Changes in `0.48.0` and `0.48.1`
  8‚Üí
  9‚ÜíStarting with Ktlint `0.48.x`, rule and rule sets can be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
 10‚Üí
 11‚ÜíAPI Consumers that provide experimental rules to the KtLintRuleEngine, must also enable the experimental rules or instruct their users to do so in the `.editorconfig` file. From the perspective of the API Consumer it might be confusing or unnecessary to do so as the experimental rule was already provided explicitly.
 12‚Üí
 13‚ÜíKtlint wants to provide the user (e.g. a developer) a uniform and consistent user experience. The `.editorconfig` becomes more and more central to store configuration for Ktlint. This to ensure that all team members use the exact same configuration when running ktlint regardless whether the Ktlint CLI or an API Consumer is being used.
 14‚Üí
 15‚ÜíThe `.editorconfig` is a powerful configuration tool which can be used in very different ways. Most projects use a single `.editorconfig` file containing one common section for kotlin and kotlin scripts files. For example, the `.editorconfig` file of the Ktlint project contains following section:
 16‚Üí```editorconfig
 17‚Üí[*.{kt,kts}]
 18‚Üíij_kotlin_imports_layout = *
 19‚Üíij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma = true
 20‚Üíij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = true
 21‚Üí```
 22‚ÜíOther projects might contain multiple `.editorconfig` files for different parts of the project directory hierarchy. Or, use a single `.editorconfig` file containing multiple sections with different globs. Like all other configuration settings in Ktlint, the user should be able to enable and disable the experimental rules. Both for the entire set of experimental rules and for individual experimental rules.
 23‚Üí
 24‚ÜíKtlint allows API Consumers to set default values and override values for the `.editorconfig`. Specifying a default value means that the user does not need to define the property in the `.editorconfig` file but if the user specifies the value, it will take precedence. Specifying the override value ensures that this takes precedence on a value specified by the user in the `.editorconfig`.
 25‚Üí
 26‚ÜíFrom the Ktlint perspective, it is advised that API Consumers provide the default value. See example below, for how to specify the `editorConfigDefault` property:
 27‚Üí```
 28‚ÜíKtLintRuleEngine(
 29‚Üí    ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
 30‚Üí    editorConfigDefaults = EditorConfigDefaults(
 31‚Üí        EditorConfig
 32‚Üí            .builder()
 33‚Üí            .section(
 34‚Üí                Section
 35‚Üí                    .builder()
 36‚Üí                    .glob(Glob("*.{kt,kts}"))
 37‚Üí                    .properties(
 38‚Üí                        Property
 39‚Üí                            .builder()
 40‚Üí                            .name("ktlint_experimental")
 41‚Üí                            .value("enabled"),
 42‚Üí                    ),
 43‚Üí            )
 44‚Üí            .build()
 45‚Üí    )
 46‚Üí)
 47‚Üí```
 48‚ÜíIf the user has set property `ktlint_experimental` explicitly than that value will be used. If the value is not defined, the value provided via `editorConfigDefaults` will be used.
 49‚Üí
 50‚ÜíIf you do want to ignore the value of `ktlint_experimental` as set by the user, than you can set the EditorConfigOverride property. But as said before that is discouraged as the user might not understand why the `.editorconfig` property is being ignored (provided that the value set is not equal to the value provided by the API Consumer).

 85‚Üí## [0.48.0] - 2022-12-15
 86‚Üí
 87‚Üí### Indent rule
 88‚Üí
 89‚ÜíThe `indent` rule has been rewritten from scratch. Solving problems in the old algorithm was very difficult. With the new algorithm this becomes a lot easier. Although the new implementation of the rule has been compared against several open source projects containing over 400,000 lines of code, it is still likely that new issues will be discovered. Please report your indentation issues so that these can be fixed as well.
 90‚Üí
 91‚Üí### `.editorconfig` property to disable rules
 92‚Üí
 93‚ÜíIn the previous release (0.47.x), the `.editorconfig`  property `disabled_rules` was deprecated and replaced with `ktlint_disabled_rules`. This latter property has now been deprecated as well in favour of a more flexible and better maintainable solution. Rule and rule sets can now be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
 94‚Üí
 95‚ÜíThe KtLint CLI has not been changed. Although you can still use parameter `--experimental` to enable KtLint's Experimental rule set, you might want to set `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental = enabled` instead.
 96‚Üí
 97‚Üí### API Changes & RuleSet providers
 98‚Üí
 99‚ÜíIf you are not an API consumer or Rule Set provider then you can skip this section.
100‚Üí
101‚Üí#### Class relocations
102‚Üí
103‚ÜíClasses below have been relocated:
104‚Üí
105‚Üí* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties.EditorConfigProperty` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty`. 
106‚Üí* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLintParseException` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintParseException`.
107‚Üí* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleExecutionException` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintRuleException`.
108‚Üí* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.format.internal.Color` has been moved to `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.format.Color`.
109‚Üí* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain.internal.Color` has been moved to `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain.Color`.
110‚Üí
111‚Üí#### Invoking `lint` and `format`
112‚Üí
113‚ÜíThis is the last release that supports the `ExperimentalParams` to invoke the `lint` and `format` functions of KtLint. The `ExperimentalParams` contains a mix of configuration settings which are not dependent on the file/code which is to be processed. Other parameters in that class describe the code/file to be processed but can be configured inconsistently (for example a file with name "foo.kt" could be marked as a Kotlin Script file).
114‚Üí
115‚ÜíThe static object `KtLint` is deprecated and replaced by class `KtLintRuleEngine` which is configured with `KtLintRuleEngineConfiguration`. The instance of the `KtLintRuleEngine` is intended to be reused for scanning all files in a project and should not be recreated per file.
116‚Üí
117‚ÜíBoth `lint` and `format` are simplified and can now be called for a code block or for an entire file.
118‚Üí
119‚Üí```kotlin
120‚Üíimport java.io.File
121‚Üí
122‚Üí// Define a reusable instance of the KtLint Rule Engine
123‚Üíval ktLintRuleEngine = KtLintRuleEngine(
124‚Üí  // Define configuration
125‚Üí)
126‚Üí
127‚Üí
128‚Üí// Process a collection of files
129‚Üíval files: Set<File> // Collect files in a convenient way
130‚Üífiles.forEach(file in files) {
131‚Üí    ktLintRuleEngine.lint(file) {
132‚Üí        // Handle lint violations
133‚Üí    }
134‚Üí}
135‚Üí
136‚Üí// or process a code sample for a given filepath
137‚ÜíktLintRuleEngine.lint(
138‚Üí  code = "code to be linted",
139‚Üí  filePath = Path("/path/to/source/file")
140‚Üí) {
141‚Üí  // Handle lint violations
142‚Üí}
143‚Üí```
144‚Üí
145‚Üí#### Retrieve `.editorconfig`s
146‚Üí
147‚ÜíThe list of `.editorconfig` files which will be accessed by KtLint when linting or formatting a given path can now be retrieved with the new API `KtLint.editorConfigFilePaths(path: Path): List<Path>`. 
148‚Üí
149‚ÜíThis API can be called with either a file or a directory. It's intended usage is that it is called once with the root directory of a project before actually linting or formatting files of that project. When called with a directory path, all `.editorconfig` files in the directory or any of its subdirectories (except hidden directories) are returned. In case the given directory does not contain an `.editorconfig` file or if it does not contain the `root=true` setting, the parent directories are scanned as well until a root `.editorconfig` file is found.
150‚Üí
151‚ÜíCalling this API with a file path results in the `.editorconfig` files that will be accessed when processing that specific file. In case the directory in which the file resides does not contain an `.editorconfig` file or if it does not contain the `root=true` setting, the parent directories are scanned until a root `.editorconfig` file is found.
152‚Üí
153‚Üí#### Psi filename replaces FILE_PATH_USER_DATA_KEY
154‚Üí
155‚ÜíConstant `KtLint.FILE_PATH_USER_DATA_KEY` is deprecated and will be removed in KtLint version 0.49.0. The file name will be passed correctly to the node with element type FILE and can be retrieved as follows:
156‚Üí```kotlin
157‚Üíif (node.isRoot()) {
158‚Üí    val fileName = (node.psi as? KtFile)?.name
159‚Üí    ...
160‚Üí}
161‚Üí```
162‚Üí
163‚Üí### Added
164‚Üí* Wrap blocks in case the max line length is exceeded or in case the block contains a new line `wrapping` ([#1643](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1643))
165‚Üí* patterns can be read in from `stdin` with the `--patterns-from-stdin` command line options/flags ([#1606](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1606))
166‚Üí* Add basic formatting for context receiver in `indent` rule and new experimental rule `context-receiver-wrapping` ([#1672](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/1672))
167‚Üí* Add naming rules for classes and objects (`class-naming`), functions (`function-naming`) and properties (`property-naming`) ([#44](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issue/44))
168‚Üí* Add new built-in reporter `plain-summary` which prints a summary the number of violation which have been autocorrected or could not be autocorrected, both split by rule. 
169‚Üí

 20‚Üí/**
 21‚Üí * [Kotlin lang documentation](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#source-file-names):
 22‚Üí * If a Kotlin file contains a single class (potentially with related top-level declarations), its name should be
 23‚Üí * the same as the name of the class, with the `.kt` extension appended. If a file contains multiple classes,
 24‚Üí * or only top-level declarations, choose a name describing what the file contains, and name the file accordingly.
 25‚Üí * Use upper camel case with an uppercase first letter (also known as Pascal case),
 26‚Üí * for example, `ProcessDeclarations.kt`.
 27‚Üí *
 28‚Üí * According to issue https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KTIJ-21897/Kotlin-coding-convention-file-naming-for-class,
 29‚Üí * "class" above should be read as any type of class (data class, enum class, sealed class) and interfaces.
 30‚Üí *
 31‚Üí * A strict implementation of guideline above had unwanted consequences:
 32‚Üí *   - If the file contains a single top level private class, it does not make sense to force the name of file to be
 33‚Üí *     identical to that class.
 34‚Üí *   - If the file contains a coherent set of functions and one of those function returns an instance of a public class
 35‚Üí *     which happens to be the only top level class in that file, it might not always be best to force the file to be
 36‚Üí *     named after that class.
 37‚Üí *   - Existing functionality regarding files containing a single top level object/typealias was lost.
 38‚Üí *
 39‚Üí * Exceptions to this rule:
 40‚Üí * - file without `.kt` extension
 41‚Üí * - file with name `package.kt`
 42‚Üí */
 43‚Üípublic class FilenameRule : Rule("filename") {
 44‚Üí    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 45‚Üí        node: ASTNode,
 46‚Üí        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 47‚Üí        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 48‚Üí    ) {
 49‚Üí        if (node.isRoot()) {
 50‚Üí            node as FileASTNode? ?: error("node is not ${FileASTNode::class} but ${node::class}")
 51‚Üí
 52‚Üí            val filePath = (node.psi as? KtFile)?.virtualFilePath
 53‚Üí            if (filePath?.endsWith(".kt") != true || filePath.endsWith("package.kt")) {
 54‚Üí                // ignore all non ".kt" files (including ".kts")
 55‚Üí                stopTraversalOfAST()
 56‚Üí                return
 57‚Üí            }
 58‚Üí
 59‚Üí            val fileName =
 60‚Üí                filePath
 61‚Üí                    .replace('\\', '/') // Ensure compatibility with Windows OS
 62‚Üí                    .substringAfterLast("/")
 63‚Üí                    .substringBefore(".")
 64‚Üí
 65‚Üí            val topLevelClassDeclarations = node.topLevelDeclarations(CLASS)
 66‚Üí            if (topLevelClassDeclarations.size == 1) {
 67‚Üí                val topLevelClassDeclaration = topLevelClassDeclarations.first()
 68‚Üí                if (node.hasTopLevelDeclarationNotExtending(topLevelClassDeclaration.identifier)) {
 69‚Üí                    fileName.shouldMatchPascalCase(emit)
 70‚Üí                } else {
 71‚Üí                    // If the file only contains one (non-private) top level class and possibly some extension functions of
 72‚Üí                    // that class, then its filename should be identical to the class name.
 73‚Üí                    fileName.shouldMatchClassName(topLevelClassDeclaration.identifier, emit)
 74‚Üí                }
 75‚Üí            } else {
 76‚Üí                val topLevelDeclarations = node.topLevelDeclarations()
 77‚Üí                if (topLevelDeclarations.size == 1) {
 78‚Üí                    val topLevelDeclaration = topLevelDeclarations.first()
 79‚Üí                    if (topLevelDeclaration.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ||
 80‚Üí                        topLevelDeclaration.elementType == TYPEALIAS
 81‚Üí                    ) {
 82‚Üí                        val pascalCaseIdentifier =
 83‚Üí                            topLevelDeclaration
 84‚Üí                                .identifier
 85‚Üí                                .toPascalCase()
 86‚Üí                        fileName.shouldMatchFileName(pascalCaseIdentifier, emit)
 87‚Üí                    } else {
 88‚Üí                        fileName.shouldMatchPascalCase(emit)
 89‚Üí                    }
 90‚Üí                } else {
 91‚Üí                    fileName.shouldMatchPascalCase(emit)
 92‚Üí                }
 93‚Üí            }
 94‚Üí            stopTraversalOfAST()
 95‚Üí        }
 96‚Üí    }
 97‚Üí
 98‚Üí    private fun ASTNode.topLevelDeclarations(elementType: IElementType? = null): List<TopLevelDeclaration> =
 99‚Üí        children()
100‚Üí            .filter { elementType == null || it.elementType == elementType }
101‚Üí            .filter { it.doesNotHavePrivateModifier() }
102‚Üí            .mapNotNull { it.toTopLevelDeclaration() }
103‚Üí            .distinct()
104‚Üí            .toList()
105‚Üí
106‚Üí    private fun ASTNode.doesNotHavePrivateModifier(): Boolean =
107‚Üí        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
108‚Üí            ?.children()
109‚Üí            ?.none { it.text == "private" }
110‚Üí            ?: true
111‚Üí
112‚Üí    private fun ASTNode.hasTopLevelDeclarationNotExtending(className: String) =
113‚Üí        children()
114‚Üí            .filter { it.doesNotHavePrivateModifier() }
115‚Üí            .any { it.isNotClassRelatedTopLevelDeclaration() || it.isFunctionNotExtending(className) }
116‚Üí
117‚Üí    private fun ASTNode.isNotClassRelatedTopLevelDeclaration() =
118‚Üí        elementType in NON_CLASS_RELATED_TOP_LEVEL_DECLARATION_TYPES
119‚Üí
120‚Üí    private fun ASTNode.isFunctionNotExtending(className: String) =
121‚Üí        elementType == FUN &&
122‚Üí            findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)?.text?.let { !it.contains(className) } ?: true
123‚Üí
124‚Üí    private fun String.shouldMatchClassName(
125‚Üí        className: String,
126‚Üí        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
127‚Üí    ) {
128‚Üí        if (this != className) {
129‚Üí            emit(
130‚Üí                0,
131‚Üí                "File '$this.kt' contains a single class and possibly also extension functions for that class and should be named same after that class '$className.kt'",
132‚Üí                false,
133‚Üí            )
134‚Üí        }
135‚Üí    }
136‚Üí
137‚Üí    private fun String.toPascalCase() =
138‚Üí        replaceFirstChar { it.uppercaseChar() }
139‚Üí
140‚Üí    private fun String.shouldMatchFileName(
141‚Üí        filename: String,
142‚Üí        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
143‚Üí    ) {
144‚Üí        if (this != filename) {
145‚Üí            emit(
146‚Üí                0,
147‚Üí                "File '$this.kt' contains a single top level declaration and should be named '$filename.kt'",
148‚Üí                false,
149‚Üí            )
150‚Üí        }
151‚Üí    }
152‚Üí
153‚Üí    private fun String.shouldMatchPascalCase(
154‚Üí        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
155‚Üí    ) {
156‚Üí        if (!this.matches(PASCAL_CASE_REGEX)) {
157‚Üí            emit(0, "File name '$this.kt' should conform PascalCase", false)
158‚Üí        }

160‚Üí
161‚Üí    private data class TopLevelDeclaration(
162‚Üí        val elementType: IElementType,
163‚Üí        val identifier: String,
164‚Üí    )
165‚Üí
166‚Üí    private fun ASTNode.toTopLevelDeclaration(): TopLevelDeclaration? =
167‚Üí        findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
168‚Üí            ?.text
169‚Üí            ?.removeSurrounding("`")
170‚Üí            ?.let { TopLevelDeclaration(elementType, it) }
